sections.title: Patient at a Glance
body_markdown_lines: | Parameter | Value |
| **Age / Sex** | 59-year-old male |
| **Smoking history** | Ex-smoker (30 pack-year history, ceased 4 years prior) |
| **Histology** | Non-small cell lung carcinoma (adenocarcinoma, PD-L1 TPS 40%) |
| **Stage** | T3 N2 M0 (AJCC 8th ed. Stage IIIB) — bulky left lower lobe primary (6.2 cm) with ipsilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy (station 7, short axis 2.1 cm) |
| **ECOG Performance Status** | 2 |
| **Pulmonary function** | Severe COPD — FEV₁ 0.9 L (38% predicted), DLCO 42% predicted, home O₂ at rest |
| **Relevant comorbidities** | Active autoimmune hepatitis on low-dose prednisolone and azathioprine; ischaemic heart disease (CABG × 3, 2019) |
| **Presenting symptoms** | Haemoptysis (daily for 3 weeks), persistent productive cough, progressive exertional dyspnoea (mMRC 3) |
| **MDT recommendation** | Not suitable for definitive concurrent chemoradiotherapy or immunotherapy. Recommended high-dose palliative RT for local symptom control and durable disease suppression. |
| **Prescription** | 40 Gy in 10 daily fractions (BED₁₀ = 56.0 Gy) |
| **Technique** | Volumetric modulated arc therapy (VMAT) |
| **Concurrent systemic therapy** | Nil — assume no concurrent systemic therapy for this challenge |

sections.title: Treatment Approach
body_markdown_lines: This case uses a **standard-fractionation high-dose palliative regimen** (40 Gy / 10 fx, BED₁₀ 56.0 Gy) rather than a stereotactic approach. The distinction is clinically important and drives the planning constraints.
**Why this regimen was chosen:**

The 40 Gy / 10 fx schedule bridges the gap between conventional low-dose palliation (e.g. 20 Gy / 5 fx) and definitive-dose treatment. It offers a higher probability of durable local control without the OAR dose intensity of a definitive regimen — appropriate for a patient whose comorbidity profile (severe COPD, immunosuppression) precludes concurrent systemic therapy.
**Planning implications:**
- OAR constraints are derived from conventional fractionation tolerances, not SBRT-specific limits.
- The larger PTV and conventional margins mean the planner must manage broad low-dose wash and competing serial OAR priorities simultaneously.
- The goal is a reproducible, peer-reviewable VMAT plan that balances coverage against OAR protection — not maximal ablative dose.
See **Supplementary** for the full regimen rationale and evidence base.
